title: Asking Questions & Directions Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Welcome! 👋 Unit 7 — Asking Questions and Directions.
Three things this unit: a **directions dialogue**, all eight **question words**, and the polite way to ask for and follow directions. Short and practical. Let's go!
Picture this: you're in a Maltese town, you need the bank. You stop a passer-by. Here's how it goes.
Excuse me, where is the bank? Short. Polite. Clear. 🙋
The reply — go straight, then turn right:

transcript.mt: Mur dritt, imbagħad dur fuq il-lemin.

transcript.en: The good news — no, just five minutes:
Thank you very much! Perfect exchange. Five lines and you're sorted. ☕
Eight little words that unlock almost any question in Maltese. 🗝️
Also said with a longer form. Example:

transcript.mt: Xi trid tixrob, kapuċċino jew te?

transcript.en: What do you want to drink, a cappuccino or tea?
Also said in a shorter form. Example:
How do we get to the airport?
Put any of these at the front of a sentence and you've got a question. Eight words, endless conversations. 🎯
The polite, foolproof way to ask for directions. This opener works every time:

transcript.mt: Skużani. Tista' tgħidli fejn hi l-iskola, jekk jogħġbok?

transcript.en: Excuse me. Can you tell me where the school is, please? Note: the opener there means 'Can you' — that's the polite request form. 💡
Match the gender of the place: use the feminine form for places like the school, and the masculine form for places like the hospital:

transcript.mt: Skużi. Tista' tgħidli fejn hu l-isptar, jekk jogħġbok?

transcript.en: Excuse me. Can you tell me where the hospital is, please? 🏥
Now the direction words you'll hear in the answer. Right:
A full reply might sound like this — walk to the square, turn left, and there you'll see it opposite the church:

transcript.mt: Imxi sal-pjazza, dur fuq ix-xellug. Hemmhekk tarah faċċata tal-knisja.

transcript.en: Keep going straight and you'll see it on the right:

transcript.mt: Ibqa' sejjer dritt u taraha fuq il-lemin

transcript.en: Bonus: if you love the sea or the weather forecast, Maltese wind directions are a lovely extra. 🌬️
Plus four diagonals for north-east, south-east, south-west, and north-west. Always capitalised in writing.
Directions dialogue. Eight question words. Asking your way politely. That's Unit 7.
Three of those eight question words — 'who', 'where', and 'how' — will handle 80% of your daily questions. Start there. 💪
